model.uncertainty: medium for read-heavy no-auto-import/no-auto-flush workloads; high for write-heavy or dirty-sync workloads until contention replay evidence is paired

high_core_host.guidance: Read-heavy swarms at or below one br read command per agent per second should stay comfortably below the 500 ms p95 budget on this host class, assuming dirty_count stays 0.
Allowed for bursts or monitored runs. Re-run NUMA profile and enable sequential drift guard invalidation when p95 read latency exceeds the 5% budget or corpus hash changes.
Do not treat as safe without contention replay and write-lock evidence. Page-read/futex floors and scheduler service CPU are likely to dominate.

laptop_or_small_vm.guidance: Use no-auto-import/no-auto-flush for read-heavy loops and keep sync explicit.
Expect visible p95 jitter; prefer batching status checks and reducing scheduler/list polling cadence.
Move br-heavy swarm work to a high-core host or use a shared operator process/report artifact instead of per-agent command storms.

rerun_when: workspace dirty_count is nonzero before the run
jsonl hash or issue_count changes materially
default p95 for weighted mix regresses by more than 5 percent
host has multiple NUMA nodes and cross-node profile has not been captured
invalidation.protected_by: SequentialDriftGuard can disable adaptive recommendations when evidence drifts or proof obligations fail.
